arts.ttl: Scott McTominay opens the scoring after just 25 SECONDS for Napoli against Como as former Man United midfielder impresses in Italy
arts.sum: After receiving the ball from former Manchester United team-mate Romelu Lukaku, McTominay showed impressive control before finishing well.

arts.ttl: Napoli director Manna: ‘We’re talking to Kvara about contract renewal, but there’s no rush’
arts.sum: Napoli sporting director Giovanni Manna has provided an update on the contract situation of star winger Khvicha Kvaratskhelia. Speaking to the media before Napoli’s match against Como, Manna emphasized that there is no rush to finalize a new deal with the Georgian international. “The situation with Kvara is very clear: he has three years left on his contract. We are talking, but we have no stress at this moment. We are focused on many other things. We will calmly discuss with him and his agent, but without anxiety,” Manna explained. Osvaldo reveals smoking room at Juventus, discusses depression battle and near-move to Napoli
arts.sum: In a candid interview with Tyc Sport in Argentina, former striker Pablo Daniel Osvaldo has made some surprising revelations about his time in Italian football, particularly at Juventus. The ex-Roma, Inter, and Fiorentina player also opened up about his recent struggle with depression and a potential move to Napoli that never materialized. Osvaldo, who spent six months at Juventus from January to June 2014, disclosed the existence of a designated smoking area for players: “When I was at Juventus, we had our own smoking room. arts.ttl: Osimhen bomb! 'First yes' for transfer deal
arts.sum: Napoli would be open to selling Victor Osimhen to Serie A rivals Juventus, but there is a problem, according to Italian media. As a result, the names of other clubs have been bandied about in the media, with Napoli's Serie A rivals Juventus the latest side to be linked with Osimhen.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Napoli v Como: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: As Serie A leaders Napoli prepare to host Como at the Stadio Maradona, both teams are expected to maintain their winning lineups from recent matches. Antonio Conte’s Napoli currently hold a slim one-point advantage over Juventus at the top of the table, having gone undefeated in their last five league encounters. Meanwhile, Cesc Fabregas’ Como side enters the match with momentum, having secured victories in their previous two Serie A outings, including an impressive 3-2 away victory over Atalanta in Bergamo.
